title: Clock, Delta Time, Fixed Steps, Interpolation, and Stalls · Language Clinic: Stall Clamp
summary: Movement is tuned per frame, a long pause launches the ship across the map, collision results change with monitor refresh rate, and catch-up updates freeze the process. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stall clamp increment in bounded deterministic time-step policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Clamp pathological frame time and cap catch-up updates while recording discarded debt and player impact.
Accumulate real time into bounded fixed simulation updates and preserve remainder for interpolation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-time-step-simulation-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-time-step-simulation-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_time_step_simulation_3(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-stall-clamp-1kzg5d
# Competency: asteroids-stall-clamp.
# Case 1039: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence.
def evidence_asteroids_stall_clamp_1kzg5d(frame_seconds: float = 0.016, max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1, fixed_step: float = 1 / 120, accumulator: float = 0.0, max_updates: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1kzg5d = "1kzg5d-d5gzk1-1039"
    assert evidence_variant_1kzg5d
    evidence_axes_1kzg5d = (
        "player1kzg5d",
        "seed1039",
        "inputd5gzk1",
        "frame1kzg5d1039",
        "state10391kzg5d",
        "playtestd5gzk11039",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1kzg5d)) == 6
    if frame_seconds < 0.0 or fixed_step <= 0.0:
        return False, frame_seconds, "time-domain"
    clamped = min(frame_seconds, max_frame_seconds)
    updates = int((accumulator + clamped) / fixed_step)
    if updates > max_updates:
        return False, updates, "spiral-guard"
    if clamped > max_frame_seconds:
        return False, clamped, "clamp-failure"
    assert 0 <= updates <= max_updates
    return True, updates, "bounded-time-step"
